samples.reference: CONCORD RETURNED TO ITS PLACE AMIDST THE TENTS
samples.hypothesis: Concorde returned to its place amidst the tents.

samples.reference: THE ENGLISH FORWARDED TO THE FRENCH BASKETS OF FLOWERS OF WHICH THEY HAD MADE A PLENTIFUL PROVISION TO GREET THE ARRIVAL OF THE YOUNG PRINCESS THE FRENCH IN RETURN INVITED THE ENGLISH TO A SUPPER WHICH WAS TO BE GIVEN THE NEXT DAY
samples.hypothesis: The English forwarded to the French baskets of flowers of which they had made a plentiful provision to greet the arrival of the young princess. The French in return invited the English to a supper which was to be given the next day.

samples.reference: CONGRATULATIONS WERE POURED IN UPON THE PRINCESS EVERYWHERE DURING HER JOURNEY
samples.hypothesis: Congratulations were poured in upon the princess everywhere during her journey.

samples.reference: FROM THE RESPECT PAID HER ON ALL SIDES SHE SEEMED LIKE A QUEEN AND FROM THE ADORATION WITH WHICH SHE WAS TREATED BY TWO OR THREE SHE APPEARED AN OBJECT OF WORSHIP THE QUEEN MOTHER GAVE THE FRENCH THE MOST AFFECTIONATE RECEPTION FRANCE WAS HER NATIVE COUNTRY AND SHE HAD SUFFERED TOO MUCH UNHAPPINESS IN ENGLAND FOR ENGLAND TO HAVE MADE HER FORGET FRANCE
samples.hypothesis: From the respect paid her on all sides, she seemed like a queen, and from the adoration with which she was treated by two or three, she appeared an object of worship. The queen mother gave the French the most affectionate reception. France was her native country, and she had suffered too much unhappiness in England for England to have made her forget France.

samples.reference: SHE TAUGHT HER DAUGHTER THEN BY HER OWN AFFECTION FOR IT THAT LOVE FOR A COUNTRY WHERE THEY HAD BOTH BEEN HOSPITABLY RECEIVED AND WHERE A BRILLIANT FUTURE OPENED BEFORE THEM
samples.hypothesis: She taught her daughter then by her own affection for it that love for a country where they had both been hospitably received and where a brilliant future opened for them.

samples.reference: THE COUNT HAD THROWN HIMSELF BACK ON HIS SEAT LEANING HIS SHOULDERS AGAINST THE PARTITION OF THE TENT AND REMAINED THUS HIS FACE BURIED IN HIS HANDS WITH HEAVING CHEST AND RESTLESS LIMBS
samples.hypothesis: The count had thrown himself back on his seat, leaning his shoulders against the partition of the tent, and remained thus, his face buried in his hands, with heaving chest and restless limbs.

samples.reference: THIS HAS INDEED BEEN A HARASSING DAY CONTINUED THE YOUNG MAN HIS EYES FIXED UPON HIS FRIEND
samples.hypothesis: This has indeed been a harassing day," continued the young man, his eyes fixed upon his friend.

samples.reference: YOU WILL BE FRANK WITH ME I ALWAYS AM
samples.hypothesis: You will be frank with me, I always am.

samples.reference: CAN YOU IMAGINE WHY BUCKINGHAM HAS BEEN SO VIOLENT I SUSPECT
samples.hypothesis: Can you imagine why Buckingham has been so violent? I suspect.

samples.reference: IT IS YOU WHO ARE MISTAKEN RAOUL I HAVE READ HIS DISTRESS IN HIS EYES IN HIS EVERY GESTURE AND ACTION THE WHOLE DAY
samples.hypothesis: It is you who are mistaken, Raoul. I have read his distress in his eyes, in his every gesture and action the whole day.

samples.reference: I CAN PERCEIVE LOVE CLEARLY ENOUGH
samples.hypothesis: I can perceive love clearly enough.

samples.reference: I AM CONVINCED OF WHAT I SAY SAID THE COUNT
samples.hypothesis: I am convinced of what I say," said the Count.

samples.reference: IT IS ANNOYANCE THEN
samples.hypothesis: It is annoyance then.

samples.reference: IN THOSE VERY TERMS I EVEN ADDED MORE
samples.hypothesis: In those very terms, I even added more.

samples.reference: BUT CONTINUED RAOUL NOT INTERRUPTED BY THIS MOVEMENT OF HIS FRIEND HEAVEN BE PRAISED THE FRENCH WHO ARE PRONOUNCED TO BE THOUGHTLESS AND INDISCREET RECKLESS EVEN ARE CAPABLE OF BRINGING A CALM AND SOUND JUDGMENT TO BEAR ON MATTERS OF SUCH HIGH IMPORTANCE
samples.hypothesis: But, continued Raoul, not interrupted by this movement of his friend, "Heaven be praised! The French, who are pronounced to be thoughtless and indiscreet, reckless even, are capable of bringing a calm and sound judgment to bear on matters of such high import."

samples.reference: THUS IT IS THAT THE HONOR OF THREE IS SAVED OUR COUNTRY'S OUR MASTER'S AND OUR OWN
samples.hypothesis: Thus it is that the honor of three is saved: our country, our masters, and our own.

samples.reference: YES I NEED REPOSE MANY THINGS HAVE AGITATED ME TO DAY BOTH IN MIND AND BODY WHEN YOU RETURN TO MORROW I SHALL NO LONGER BE THE SAME MAN
samples.hypothesis: Yes, I need repose. Many things have agitated me today, both in mind and body. When you return to morrow, I shall no longer be the same man.

samples.reference: BUT IN THIS FRIENDLY PRESSURE RAOUL COULD DETECT THE NERVOUS AGITATION OF A GREAT INTERNAL CONFLICT
samples.hypothesis: But in this friendly pressure, Raoul could detect the nervous agitation of a great internal conflict.

samples.reference: THE NIGHT WAS CLEAR STARLIT AND SPLENDID THE TEMPEST HAD PASSED AWAY AND THE SWEET INFLUENCES OF THE EVENING HAD RESTORED LIFE PEACE AND SECURITY EVERYWHERE
samples.hypothesis: The night was clear, starlit, and splendid. The tempest had passed away, and the sweet influences of the evening had restored life, peace, and security everywhere.

samples.reference: UPON THE LARGE SQUARE IN FRONT OF THE HOTEL THE SHADOWS OF THE TENTS INTERSECTED BY THE GOLDEN MOONBEAMS FORMED AS IT WERE A HUGE MOSAIC OF JET AND YELLOW FLAGSTONES
samples.hypothesis: Upon the large square in front of the hotel, the shadows of the tents intersected by the golden moonbeams formed, as it were, a huge mosaic of jet and yellow flagstones.

samples.reference: BRAGELONNE WATCHED FOR SOME TIME THE CONDUCT OF THE TWO LOVERS LISTENED TO THE LOUD AND UNCIVIL SLUMBERS OF MANICAMP WHO SNORED AS IMPERIOUSLY AS THOUGH HE WAS WEARING HIS BLUE AND GOLD INSTEAD OF HIS VIOLET SUIT
samples.hypothesis: Bragelonne watched for some time the conduct of the two lovers, listened to the loud and uncivil slumbers of Manicamp, who snored as imperiously as though he was wearing his blue and gold instead of his violet suit.

samples.reference: GOLIATH MAKES ANOTHER DISCOVERY
samples.hypothesis: Goliath makes another discovery.

samples.reference: THEY WERE CERTAINLY NO NEARER THE SOLUTION OF THEIR PROBLEM
samples.hypothesis: They were certainly no nearer the solution of their problem.

samples.reference: THE POOR LITTLE THINGS CRIED CYNTHIA THINK OF THEM HAVING BEEN TURNED TO THE WALL ALL THESE YEARS
samples.hypothesis: The poor little things cried. Cynthia, think of them having been turned to the wall all these years.

samples.reference: NOW WHAT WAS THE SENSE OF IT TWO INNOCENT BABIES LIKE THAT
samples.hypothesis: Now what is the sense of it? Two innocent babies like that.

samples.reference: BUT JOYCE HAD NOT BEEN LISTENING ALL AT ONCE SHE PUT DOWN HER CANDLE ON THE TABLE AND FACED HER COMPANION
samples.hypothesis: But Joyce had not been listening. All at once, she put down her candle on the table and faced her companion.

samples.reference: THE TWIN BROTHER DID SOMETHING SHE DIDN'T LIKE AND SHE TURNED HIS PICTURE TO THE WALL
samples.hypothesis: The twin brother did something she didn't like, and she turned his picture to the wall.

samples.reference: HERS HAPPENED TO BE IN THE SAME FRAME TOO BUT SHE EVIDENTLY DIDN'T CARE ABOUT THAT
samples.hypothesis: Hers happened to be on the same frame too, but she evidently didn't care about it.

samples.reference: NOW WHAT HAVE YOU TO SAY CYNTHIA SPRAGUE
samples.hypothesis: Now what have you to say, Cynthia Sprague?

samples.reference: I THOUGHT WE WERE STUMPED AGAIN WHEN I FIRST SAW THAT PICTURE BUT IT'S BEEN OF SOME USE AFTER ALL
samples.hypothesis: I thought we were stumped again when I first saw that picture, but it's been of some use after all.

samples.reference: DO YOU SUPPOSE THE MINIATURE WAS A COPY OF THE SAME THING
samples.hypothesis: Do you suppose the miniature was a copy of the same thing?

samples.reference: WHAT IN THE WORLD IS THAT QUERIED JOYCE
samples.hypothesis: What in the world is it? Queried Joyce.

samples.reference: THEY WORRY ME TERRIBLY AND BESIDES I'D LIKE TO SEE WHAT THIS LOVELY FURNITURE LOOKS LIKE WITHOUT SUCH QUANTITIES OF DUST ALL OVER IT GOOD SCHEME CYN
samples.hypothesis: They worry me terribly, and besides, I'd like to see what this lovely furniture looks like without such quantities of dust all over it. Good scheme, Sam.

samples.reference: WE'LL COME IN HERE THIS AFTERNOON WITH OLD CLOTHES ON AND HAVE A REGULAR HOUSE CLEANING
samples.hypothesis: We'll come in here this afternoon with old clothes on and have a regular house cleaning.

samples.reference: IT CAN'T HURT ANYTHING I'M SURE FOR WE WON'T DISTURB THINGS AT ALL
samples.hypothesis: It can't hurt anything, I'm sure. For we won't disturb things at all.

samples.reference: THIS THOUGHT HOWEVER DID NOT ENTER THE HEADS OF THE ENTHUSIASTIC PAIR
samples.hypothesis: This thought, however, did not enter the heads of the enthusiastic pair.

samples.reference: SMUGGLING THE HOUSE CLEANING PARAPHERNALIA INTO THE CELLAR WINDOW UNOBSERVED THAT AFTERNOON PROVED NO EASY TASK FOR CYNTHIA HAD ADDED A WHISK BROOM AND DUST PAN TO THE OUTFIT
samples.hypothesis: Smuggling the house cleaning paraphernalia into the cellar window unobserved that afternoon proved no easy task for Cynthia. Had added a whisk broom and dustpan to the outfit.

samples.reference: THE LURE PROVED TOO MUCH FOR HIM AND HE CAME SPORTING AFTER IT AS FRISKILY AS A YOUNG KITTEN MUCH TO CYNTHIA'S DELIGHT WHEN SHE CAUGHT SIGHT OF HIM
samples.hypothesis: The lure proved too much for him, and he came sporting after it as friskily as a young kitten, much to Cynthia's delight when she caught sight of him.

samples.reference: OH LET HIM COME ALONG SHE URGED I DO LOVE TO SEE HIM ABOUT THAT OLD HOUSE
samples.hypothesis: Oh, let him come along," she urged. "I do love to see him about that old house.

samples.reference: HE MAKES IT SORT OF COZIER
samples.hypothesis: He makes it sort of cosier.

samples.reference: NOW LET'S DUST THE FURNITURE AND PICTURES
samples.hypothesis: Now let's dust the furniture and pictures.

samples.reference: YET LITTLE AS IT WAS IT HAD ALREADY MADE A VAST DIFFERENCE IN THE ASPECT OF THE ROOM
samples.hypothesis: Yet, little as it was, it had already made a vast difference in the aspect of the room.

samples.reference: SURFACE DUST AT LEAST HAD BEEN REMOVED AND THE FINE OLD FURNITURE GAVE A HINT OF ITS REAL ELEGANCE AND POLISH
samples.hypothesis: Surface dust, at least, had been removed, and the fine old furniture gave a hint of its real elegance and polish.

samples.reference: THEN SHE SUDDENLY REMARKED
samples.hypothesis: Then she suddenly remarked.

samples.reference: AND MY POCKET MONEY IS GETTING LOW AGAIN AND YOU HAVEN'T ANY LEFT AS USUAL
samples.hypothesis: And my pocket money is getting low again, and you haven't any left as usual.

samples.reference: THEY SAY ILLUMINATION BY CANDLE LIGHT IS THE PRETTIEST IN THE WORLD
samples.hypothesis: They say illumination by candlelight is the prettiest in the world.

samples.reference: WHY IT'S GOLIATH AS USUAL THEY BOTH CRIED PEERING IN
samples.hypothesis: Why it's Goliath as usual. They both cried peering in.

samples.reference: ISN'T HE THE GREATEST FOR GETTING INTO ODD CORNERS
samples.hypothesis: Isn't he the greatest for getting into odd corners.

samples.reference: FORGETTING ALL THEIR WEARINESS THEY SEIZED THEIR CANDLES AND SCURRIED THROUGH THE HOUSE FINDING AN OCCASIONAL PAPER TUCKED AWAY IN SOME ODD CORNER
samples.hypothesis: Forgetting all their weariness, they seized their candles and scurried through the house, finding on occasional paper tucked away in some odd corner.

samples.reference: WELL I'M CONVINCED THAT THE BOARDED UP HOUSE MYSTERY HAPPENED NOT EARLIER THAN APRIL SIXTEENTH EIGHTEEN SIXTY ONE AND PROBABLY NOT MUCH LATER
samples.hypothesis: Well, I am convinced that the boarded-up house mystery happened not earlier than April sixteenth, eighteen sixty one, and probably not much later.

samples.reference: NO WORDS WERE SPOKEN NO LANGUAGE WAS UTTERED SAVE THAT OF WAILING AND HISSING AND THAT SOMEHOW WAS INDISTINCT AS IF IT EXISTED IN FANCY AND NOT IN REALITY
samples.hypothesis: No words were spoken, no language was uttered, save that of wailing and hissing, and that somehow was indistinct, as if it existed in fancy and not in reality.

samples.reference: I HEARD A NOISE BEHIND I TURNED AND SAW KAFFAR HIS BLACK EYES SHINING WHILE IN HIS HAND HE HELD A GLEAMING KNIFE HE LIFTED IT ABOVE HIS HEAD AS IF TO STRIKE BUT I HAD THE STRENGTH OF TEN MEN AND I HURLED HIM FROM ME
samples.hypothesis: I heard a noise behind. I turned and saw Kaffir, his black eyes shining, while in his hand he held a gleaming knife. He lifted it above his head as if to strike, but I had the strength of ten men and I hurled him from me.

samples.reference: ONWARD SAID A DISTANT VOICE
samples.hypothesis: Onward said a distant voice.

samples.reference: NO SOUND BROKE THE STILLNESS OF THE NIGHT
samples.hypothesis: No sound broke the stillness of the night.

samples.reference: THE STORY OF ITS EVIL INFLUENCE CAME BACK TO ME AND IN MY BEWILDERED CONDITION I WONDERED WHETHER THERE WAS NOT SOME TRUTH IN WHAT HAD BEEN SAID
samples.hypothesis: The story of its evil influence came back to me, and in my bewildered condition, I wondered whether there was not some truth in what had been said.

samples.reference: WHAT WAS THAT
samples.hypothesis: What was that?

samples.reference: WHAT THEN A HUMAN HAND LARGE AND SHAPELY APPEARED DISTINCTLY ON THE SURFACE OF THE POND
samples.hypothesis: What then? A human hand, large and shapely, appeared distinctly on the surface of the paw.

samples.reference: NOTHING MORE NOT EVEN THE WRIST TO WHICH IT MIGHT BE ATTACHED
samples.hypothesis: Nothing more, not even the wrist to which it might be attached.

samples.reference: IT DID NOT BECKON OR INDEED MOVE AT ALL IT WAS AS STILL AS THE HAND OF DEATH
samples.hypothesis: It did not beckon or indeed move at all. It was as still as the hand of death.

samples.reference: I AWOKE TO CONSCIOUSNESS FIGHTING AT FIRST IT SEEMED AS IF I WAS FIGHTING WITH A PHANTOM BUT GRADUALLY MY OPPONENT BECAME MORE REAL TO ME IT WAS KAFFAR
samples.hypothesis: I awoke to consciousness fighting. At first it seemed as if I was fighting with a phantom, but gradually my opponent became more real to me. It was Caffer.

samples.reference: A SOUND OF VOICES A FLASH OF LIGHT
samples.hypothesis: A sound of voices. A flash of light.

samples.reference: A FEELING OF FREEDOM AND I WAS AWAKE WHERE
samples.hypothesis: A feeling of freedom, and I was awake. Where?

samples.reference: SAID ANOTHER VOICE WHICH I RECOGNIZED AS VOLTAIRE'S KAFFAR
samples.hypothesis: Said another voice, which I recognized as Voltaire's, Kaffir.

samples.reference: I HAD SCARCELY KNOWN WHAT I HAD BEEN SAYING OR DOING UP TO THIS TIME BUT AS HE SPOKE I LOOKED AT MY HAND
samples.hypothesis: I had scarcely known what I had been saying or doing up to this time, but as he spoke, I looked at my hand.

samples.reference: IN THE LIGHT OF THE MOON I SAW A KNIFE RED WITH BLOOD AND MY HAND TOO WAS ALSO DISCOLOURED
samples.hypothesis: In the light of the moon, I saw a knife red with blood, and my hand too was also discolored.

samples.reference: I DO NOT KNOW I AM DAZED BEWILDERED
samples.hypothesis: I do not know. I am dazed, bewildered.

samples.reference: BUT THAT IS KAFFAR'S KNIFE
samples.hypothesis: But that is Caffar's knife.

samples.reference: I KNOW HE HAD IT THIS VERY EVENING
samples.hypothesis: I know he had it this very evening.

samples.reference: I REMEMBER SAYING HAVE WE BEEN TOGETHER
samples.hypothesis: I remembered saying, "Have we been together?"

samples.reference: VOLTAIRE PICKED UP SOMETHING FROM THE GROUND AND LOOKED AT IT
samples.hypothesis: Voltaire picked up something from the ground and looked at it.

samples.reference: I SAY YOU DO KNOW WHAT THIS MEANS AND YOU MUST TELL US
samples.hypothesis: I say you do know what this means, and you must tell us.

samples.reference: A TERRIBLE THOUGHT FLASHED INTO MY MIND
samples.hypothesis: A terrible thought flashed into my mind.

samples.reference: I HAD AGAIN BEEN ACTING UNDER THE INFLUENCE OF THIS MAN'S POWER
samples.hypothesis: I had again been acting under the influence of this man's power.

samples.reference: PERCHANCE TOO KAFFAR'S DEATH MIGHT SERVE HIM IN GOOD STEAD
samples.hypothesis: Perchance too, Kaffir's death might serve him in good stead.

samples.reference: MY TONGUE REFUSED TO ARTICULATE MY POWER OF SPEECH LEFT ME
samples.hypothesis: My tongue refused to articulate. My power of speech left me.

samples.reference: MY POSITION WAS TOO TERRIBLE
samples.hypothesis: My position was too terrible.

samples.reference: MY OVERWROUGHT NERVES YIELDED AT LAST
samples.hypothesis: My overwrought nerves yielded at last.

samples.reference: FOR SOME TIME AFTER THAT I REMEMBERED NOTHING DISTINCTLY
samples.hypothesis: For some time after that, I remembered nothing distinctly.

samples.reference: NOTWITHSTANDING THE HIGH RESOLUTION OF HAWKEYE HE FULLY COMPREHENDED ALL THE DIFFICULTIES AND DANGER HE WAS ABOUT TO INCUR
samples.hypothesis: Notwithstanding the high resolution of Hawkeye, he fully comprehended all the difficulties and danger he was about to incur.

samples.reference: IN HIS RETURN TO THE CAMP HIS ACUTE AND PRACTISED INTELLECTS WERE INTENTLY ENGAGED IN DEVISING MEANS TO COUNTERACT A WATCHFULNESS AND SUSPICION ON THE PART OF HIS ENEMIES THAT HE KNEW WERE IN NO DEGREE INFERIOR TO HIS OWN
samples.hypothesis: In his return to the camp, his acute and practised intellects were intently engaged in devising means to counteract a watchfulness and suspicion on the part of his enemies, that he knew were in no degree inferior to his own.

samples.reference: IN OTHER WORDS WHILE HE HAD IMPLICIT FAITH IN THE ABILITY OF BALAAM'S ASS TO SPEAK HE WAS SOMEWHAT SKEPTICAL ON THE SUBJECT OF A BEAR'S SINGING AND YET HE HAD BEEN ASSURED OF THE LATTER ON THE TESTIMONY OF HIS OWN EXQUISITE ORGANS
samples.hypothesis: In other words, while he had implicit faith in the ability of Balaam's ass to speak, he was somewhat sceptical on the subject of a bear's singing, and yet he had been assured of the latter on the testimony of his own exquisite organs.

samples.reference: THERE WAS SOMETHING IN HIS AIR AND MANNER THAT BETRAYED TO THE SCOUT THE UTTER CONFUSION OF THE STATE OF HIS MIND
samples.hypothesis: There was something in his air and manner that betrayed to the scout the utter confusion of the state of his mind.

samples.reference: THE INGENIOUS HAWKEYE WHO RECALLED THE HASTY MANNER IN WHICH THE OTHER HAD ABANDONED HIS POST AT THE BEDSIDE OF THE SICK WOMAN WAS NOT WITHOUT HIS SUSPICIONS CONCERNING THE SUBJECT OF SO MUCH SOLEMN DELIBERATION
samples.hypothesis: The ingenious Hawkeye, who recalled the hasty manner in which the other had abandoned his post at the bedside of the sick woman, was not without his suspicions concerning the subject of so much solemn deliberation.

samples.reference: THE BEAR SHOOK HIS SHAGGY SIDES AND THEN A WELL KNOWN VOICE REPLIED
samples.hypothesis: The bear shook his shaggy sides, and then a well-known voice replied.

samples.reference: CAN THESE THINGS BE RETURNED DAVID BREATHING MORE FREELY AS THE TRUTH BEGAN TO DAWN UPON HIM
samples.hypothesis: Can these things be returned? David breathing more freely as the truth began to dawn upon him.

samples.reference: COME COME RETURNED HAWKEYE UNCASING HIS HONEST COUNTENANCE THE BETTER TO ASSURE THE WAVERING CONFIDENCE OF HIS COMPANION YOU MAY SEE A SKIN WHICH IF IT BE NOT AS WHITE AS ONE OF THE GENTLE ONES HAS NO TINGE OF RED TO IT THAT THE WINDS OF THE HEAVEN AND THE SUN HAVE NOT BESTOWED NOW LET US TO BUSINESS
samples.hypothesis: Come, come, returned Hawkeye, uncasing his honest countenance, the better to assure the wavering confidence of his companion. You may see a skin which, if it be not as white as one of the gentle ones, has no tinge of red to it that the winds of the heaven and the sun have not bestowed. Now let us to business.

samples.reference: THE YOUNG MAN IS IN BONDAGE AND MUCH I FEAR HIS DEATH IS DECREED
samples.hypothesis: The young man is in bondage, and much I fear his death is decreed.

samples.reference: I GREATLY MOURN THAT ONE SO WELL DISPOSED SHOULD DIE IN HIS IGNORANCE AND I HAVE SOUGHT A GOODLY HYMN CAN YOU LEAD ME TO HIM
samples.hypothesis: I greatly mourn that one so well disposed should die in his ignorance, and I have sought a goodly hymn. Can you lead me to him?

samples.reference: THE TASK WILL NOT BE DIFFICULT RETURNED DAVID HESITATING THOUGH I GREATLY FEAR YOUR PRESENCE WOULD RATHER INCREASE THAN MITIGATE HIS UNHAPPY FORTUNES
samples.hypothesis: The task will not be difficult," returned David, hesitating. Though I greatly fear your presence would rather increase than mitigate his unhappy fortunes.

samples.reference: THE LODGE IN WHICH UNCAS WAS CONFINED WAS IN THE VERY CENTER OF THE VILLAGE AND IN A SITUATION PERHAPS MORE DIFFICULT THAN ANY OTHER TO APPROACH OR LEAVE WITHOUT OBSERVATION
samples.hypothesis: The lodge in which Uncas was confined was in the very centre of the village and in a situation perhaps more difficult than any other to approach or leave without observation.

samples.reference: FOUR OR FIVE OF THE LATTER ONLY LINGERED ABOUT THE DOOR OF THE PRISON OF UNCAS WARY BUT CLOSE OBSERVERS OF THE MANNER OF THEIR CAPTIVE
samples.hypothesis: Four or five of the latter only lingered about the door of the prison of Uncas, wary but close observers of the manner of their captive.

samples.reference: DELIVERED IN A STRONG TONE OF ASSENT ANNOUNCED THE GRATIFICATION THE SAVAGE WOULD RECEIVE IN WITNESSING SUCH AN EXHIBITION OF WEAKNESS IN AN ENEMY SO LONG HATED AND SO MUCH FEARED
samples.hypothesis: Delivered in a strong tone of assent, announced the gratification the savage would receive in witnessing such an exhibition of weakness in an enemy so long hated and so much feared.

samples.reference: THEY DREW BACK A LITTLE FROM THE ENTRANCE AND MOTIONED TO THE SUPPOSED CONJURER TO ENTER
samples.hypothesis: They drew back a little from the entrance and motioned to the supposed conjurer to enter.

samples.reference: BUT THE BEAR INSTEAD OF OBEYING MAINTAINED THE SEAT IT HAD TAKEN AND GROWLED
samples.hypothesis: But the bear, instead of obeying, maintained the seat it had taken and growled.

samples.reference: THE CUNNING MAN IS AFRAID THAT HIS BREATH WILL BLOW UPON HIS BROTHERS AND TAKE AWAY THEIR COURAGE TOO CONTINUED DAVID IMPROVING THE HINT HE RECEIVED THEY MUST STAND FURTHER OFF
samples.hypothesis: The cunning man is afraid that his breath will blow upon his brothers and take away their courage too. Continued David, improving the hint he received, they must stand further off.

samples.reference: THEN AS IF SATISFIED OF THEIR SAFETY THE SCOUT LEFT HIS POSITION AND SLOWLY ENTERED THE PLACE
samples.hypothesis: Then, as if satisfied of their safety, the scout left his position and slowly entered the place.

samples.reference: IT WAS SILENT AND GLOOMY BEING TENANTED SOLELY BY THE CAPTIVE AND LIGHTED BY THE DYING EMBERS OF A FIRE WHICH HAD BEEN USED FOR THE PURPOSED OF COOKERY
samples.hypothesis: It was silent and gloomy, being tenanted solely by the captive and lighted by the dying embers of a fire which had been used for the purpose of cookery.

samples.reference: UNCAS OCCUPIED A DISTANT CORNER IN A RECLINING ATTITUDE BEING RIGIDLY BOUND BOTH HANDS AND FEET BY STRONG AND PAINFUL WITHES
samples.hypothesis: Uncas occupied a distant corner in a reclining attitude, being rigidly bound both hands and feet by strong and painful withes.

samples.reference: THE SCOUT WHO HAD LEFT DAVID AT THE DOOR TO ASCERTAIN THEY WERE NOT OBSERVED THOUGHT IT PRUDENT TO PRESERVE HIS DISGUISE UNTIL ASSURED OF THEIR PRIVACY
samples.hypothesis: The scout who had left David at the door to ascertain they were not observed thought it prudent to preserve his disguise until assured of their privacy.

samples.reference: WHAT SHALL WE DO WITH THE MINGOES AT THE DOOR THEY COUNT SIX AND THIS SINGER IS AS GOOD AS NOTHING
samples.hypothesis: What shall we do with the Mingoes at the door? They count six, and the singer is as good as nothing.
